signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.97 testing the 160.00 psychological and intervention threshold. Despite hawkish Fed rhetoric from Cleveland's Hammack and a +290bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%), the pair faces acute reversal risk as markets demand more than a 'token' BoJ hike. Retail sentiment is 78% short, diverging from COT LF net short -86,249; institutional carry-chasing is meeting extreme retail fading at the BoJ's 'red line'. Sustained trade above 160.00 triggers intervention warnings.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hike Bias + Vol Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1623 remains under pressure from a +175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%), but TIER-1 headlines from Commerzbank and SocGen signal an 'inevitable' ECB hike due to 3.2% services inflation. Options markets are pricing a significant 3.0 vol-pt premium (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.0%), indicating heavy hedging demand against a hawkish ECB shift. COT LF net short -26,311 suggests specs are poorly positioned for a hawkish surprise at the June 11 meeting.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/CHF G8 spread at 0.847% confirms a bilateral divergence theme as AUD outperforms (+0.464% avg) while CHF lags (-0.383% avg). The move is fundamentally anchored by a massive +435bp carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs SNB 0.0%). Retail is 84% short AUD/CHF, attempting to fade the carry-driven strength. The divergence persists as long as VIX remains at 16.0 (normal volatility), favoring carry over safe-haven CHF demand.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Pushback + CESI Support
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3459 as BoE's Bailey attributes inflation overshoot to Persian Gulf energy shocks, pushing back against 50bp hike pricing. Despite the dovish-leaning rhetoric, GBP is supported by a positive CESI (net +5 beats) and a neutral OIS carry profile vs USD (-10bp). COT LF net long +26,377 shows institutional support, though a 4.8pt VRP (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.2%) suggests the market is paying up for protection ahead of the June 18 meeting.
